schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: How to Use This to Correlate Your Photos with the Generated Vortex Images

theory.canonical_statement: **B. Build a model PSF (Airy × spike pattern) and spherical-harmonic projection** 1. Model PSF as \[ \text{PSF}(\theta,\phi) \approx \left|\frac{2J_1(\alpha)}{\alpha}\right|^2 \;+\; \sum_k A_k\, \mathrm{sinc}(\dots)\,\cos(m_k\phi+\phi_k), \] where the second term models vanes (sinc in radial coordinate, cos in angle). 2. Project the PSF onto spherical harmonics: \[ a_{\ell m}^{\text{PSF}} = \int_{S^2} \text{PSF}(\theta,\phi)\, Y_\ell^{m*}(\theta,\phi)\, d\Omega. \] 3. Similarly project your vortex model \(\Psi(\theta,\phi)\) and compute cross-correlation in spectral space: \[ C_{\ell m} = \frac{a_{\ell m}^{\text{PSF}} \; a_{\ell m}^{\Psi *}}{\sqrt{\sum |a_{\ell m}^{\text{PSF}}|^2\,\sum |a_{\ell m}^{\Psi}|^2}}. \] High \(C_{\ell m}\) for a band of \(m\) indicates those angular modes are common to both images (optical spike pattern and vortex arm structure).
theory.technical_interpretation: How to Use This to Correlate Your Photos with the Generated Vortex Images is a series / summation, written PSF(θ,φ) ≈ |(2J_1(α))/(α) |^2 + ∑_k A_k sinc(dots) cos(m_kφ+φ_k). It is expressed as a sum or series over modes or indices, superposing discrete contributions. It is built from θ, φ, α, J_1, k, A_k. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.inputs: θ, φ, α, J_1, k, A_k
